section.heading: Education and training opportunities for Coast Guard spouses

subsection.heading: Tuition Assistance.—
subsection.chapeau: The Commandant may provide, subject to the availability of appropriations, tuition assistance to an eligible spouse to facilitate the acquisition of—

paragraph.content: education and training required for a degree or credential at an accredited college, university, or technical school in the United States that expands employment and portable career opportunities for the spouse; or

education prerequisites and a professional license or credential required, by a government or government-sanctioned licensing body, for an occupation that expands employment and portable career opportunities for the spouse.

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the following definitions apply:

subparagraph.heading: In general.—
subparagraph.content: The term “eligible spouse” means the spouse of a member of the Coast Guard who is serving on active duty and includes a spouse who receives transitional compensation under section 1059 of title 10.

subparagraph.heading: Exclusion.—
subparagraph.chapeau: The term “eligible spouse” does not include an individual who—

clause.content: is married to, but legally separated from, a member of the Coast Guard under a court order or statute of any State or territorial possession of the United States; or
is eligible for tuition assistance as a member of the Armed Forces.

paragraph.heading: Portable career.—
paragraph.content: The term “portable career” includes an occupation that requires education, training, or both that results in a credential that is recognized by an industry, profession, or specific type of business.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 113–281, title II, § 214(a), Dec. 18, 2014, 128 Stat. 3030, § 542; renumbered § 2904, Pub. L. 115–282, title I, § 117(b), Dec. 4, 2018, 132 Stat. 4230; amended Pub. L. 116–283, div. G, title LVXXXV [LXXXV], § 8505(a)(49), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4750.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 2904 was renumbered section 5104 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2021—Subsec. (b)(1)(B). Pub. L. 116–283 substituted “an individual” for “a person” in introductory provisions.
2018—Pub. L. 115–282 renumbered section 542 of this title as this section.

note.heading: Second Expansion of the My Career Advancement Account Program for Military Spouses
note.p: Pub. L. 116–92, div. A, title V, § 580G, Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 1410, provided that: “The spouse of a member of the Coast Guard may participate in the My Career Advancement Account program of the Department of Defense if the Coast Guard reimburses the Department of Defense.”
